metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4624
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 6b,c. Confocal imaging of cultured rat hippocampal SDC2-overexpressing neurons at DIV2 shows localization of CASK, LIN7B and surface-NR1 (GRIN1) at the tip of dendritic spines.

Figure 7. Mutating the C2 motif of SDC2 leads to disruption of the SDC2-CASK-LIN7B-NMDAR complex, and consequently the localization of CASK, LIN7B and NR2B at the postsynaptic density was significantly reduced.

Figure 8. Disruption of the SDC2-CASK and CASK-LIN7B interactions reduces NMDA-induced calcium influx and impairs SDC2-induced spine formation.. Experimental description: "Hippocampal neurons from embryonic day 18-19 rat embryos were cultured as previously described (Lin et al., 2007; Chao et al., 2008)."

"The following antibodies and reagents were used: mouse monoclonal CASK (Millipore); ethylene glycol tetraacetic acid (EGTA) and 2-amino-5-phosphonopentanoic acid (AP5; Sigma); rabbit polyclonal LIN7 (Synaptic Systems); mouse monoclonal NMDAR1 (NR1, BD Pharmingen); rabbit polyclonal GAPDH (Santa Cruz Biotechnology); chicken polyclonal GFP (Abcam); mouse monoclonal Myc (Cell Signaling Technology); rat monoclonal HA (Roche); rabbit polyclonal GFP and Alexa Fluor 488-, 555- and 647-conjugated secondary antibodies (Invitrogen); NMDA (Tocris Bioscience)"
